title: Experiential Learning: Learning from Unstructured Actions, Observations, and Feedback
summary: Experiential Learning (EL) is a framework for training language models to learn directly from rich, token-space feedback like compiler errors and explanations rather than reducing signal to scalar rewards. On a 1B parameter model, EL boosted GSM8K accuracy from 30.4% to 39.8% and HumanEval by up to 17.6%, approaching GRPO performance while generalizing beyond verifiable domains.

title: The Supervision Horizon: An Exploration of the Mechanics of On-Policy Self-Distillation
summary: An investigation into how the supervision signal in on-policy self-distillation degrades across the completion sequence, with experiments on Qwen3 1.7B over LiveCodeBench.
